BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Quick back-of-napkin after the landlord mentioned property taxes might bump lease cost. Pre-review with Linda (CPA).

CurrentState.Note: Rough estimate only. Need to revisit with Linda once 2025 actuals are closed.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Rent increases to $750/mo if property tax passes through
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs $2,670 + $100 increase = $2,770. At $95/member, $2,770 / $95 = 29.2 → 30 members minimum.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Well within buffer at 55 members.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case - rent to $850/mo (unlikely per landlord)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. Landlord said this was unlikely but wanted to stress test.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Break-even is ~28-30 members at current cost structure. At 55 members we have plenty of headroom. Main concern was seasonal dip but even at 48 (Dec low) we're safe.
ActionItems: Wait for landlord to confirm property tax situation
Revisit after year-end with Linda for proper modeling
